bootstrap.title: First-run setup
bootstrap.subtitle: Create the initial admin — just pick a username and password; whoever submits first becomes admin.
bootstrap.displayNamePlaceholder: Shown in the header — defaults to the username

register.title: Create an account
register.subtitle: After registering you can sign in straight away.
register.subtitleOpen: After registering you can sign in straight away.
register.displayNamePlaceholder: Shown in the header — defaults to the username
register.backToLogin: Already have an account? Sign in
register.disabledHint: Open registration is currently disabled — ask an admin to invite you.

account.title: My account
account.description: Manage your proxy API keys (used by Codex to call mimo2codex) and BYOK upstream keys (used by mimo2codex to call MiMo / DeepSeek / etc. on your behalf).

apiKeys.title: My API keys
apiKeys.subtitle: Bearer tokens for the proxy. Plaintext is only shown once at creation — copy it then.
apiKeys.name: Name
apiKeys.createdAlert: Copy this now — it won't be shown again

byok.title: My upstream keys (BYOK)
byok.subtitle: When set, your personal key replaces the deployment-shared key for requests routed to that provider. Bills go to your account; other users are untouched.
byok.encrypted: Stored encrypted at rest. The plaintext is never shown again after save — keep your original copy somewhere safe.

oauth.title: OAuth providers (admin)
oauth.description: Configure OAuth credentials so users can sign in via Gitee / GitHub. Callback URLs must match what you registered with the provider (typically `https://<your-domain>/oauth/callback/<provider>`).
oauth.clientSecretKeep: Client secret (leave blank to keep existing)

codex.exportTip: Download the currently active auth.json + config.toml + a one-click apply script
codex.exportDesc: Downloads the currently active auth.json + config.toml plus a small shell / PowerShell script that applies them on your own machine (with automatic backup).
codex.exportSuccess: Downloaded — run apply.sh / apply.ps1 on your machine to apply
codex.exportDoneTitle: Export complete — what to do next
codex.exportDoneIntro: 4 files have been saved to your browser's default download directory:
codex.exportFile1: auth.json — Codex auth; the OPENAI_API_KEY field is a placeholder and must be replaced with your m2c key
codex.exportFile2: config.toml — Codex client config (base_url points at mimo2codex)
codex.exportFile3: apply-*.sh — for macOS / Linux, run with bash
codex.exportStep0: Go to My Account → My API keys, create a key and copy the plaintext
codex.exportStep1: Open auth.json and replace the `mimo2codex-local` placeholder in `OPENAI_API_KEY` with the m2c key you just copied
codex.exportStep1b: Put all 4 files in the same folder
codex.exportStep3: The script backs up your existing ~/.codex/auth.json|config.toml as *.bak.<timestamp>, then writes the new files
codex.exportStep4: Restart Codex (close and reopen the CLI / desktop app)
codex.keyEmphasisServer: In server mode the OPENAI_API_KEY in the downloaded auth.json is the placeholder `mimo2codex-local`, which is NOT a usable key. Head to My Account → My API keys, mint a key, copy the plaintext (shown exactly once at creation), and replace the placeholder in auth.json by hand.
codex.keyEmphasisServerMore: The key is visible and revocable in My Account. **Don't share it.**
codex.keyEmphasisLocal: In local mode the OPENAI_API_KEY value in auth.json is a placeholder (mimo2codex-local); mimo2codex doesn't validate it, so you can leave it alone.
codex.importTip: Upload an existing auth.json + config.toml and archive it as a new history row (rollback any time)
codex.importDesc: Paste an existing ~/.codex/auth.json + config.toml here and we'll archive it as a new history row. Local mode also writes the files to ~/.codex/; server mode just records. You can roll back from History → Bundle at any time.
codex.importGuideTitle: Where do I find these files?
codex.importGuideTip: If CODEX_HOME is set, the path follows that env var.
codex.importKeyNote: The `OPENAI_API_KEY` field inside your auth.json is archived verbatim. If you want to use mimo2codex auth, mint a new m2c key under My Account first, then put it here as `{"OPENAI_API_KEY": "m2c_xxxxxxxx"}`.
codex.exportDownloadedNote: Files have been saved to your browser's default download directory. Follow the steps above on your machine.

history.title: Codex config history
history.descServer: In server mode, click Bundle to download auth.json + config.toml + a small shell / PowerShell script that applies them on your machine (with automatic backup of existing files).
history.descLocal: In local mode the files are written to ~/.codex/ directly at apply time; History is an audit trail.
history.policy: Each user keeps the earliest initial snapshot plus the last 10 apply / restore events.
history.bundleSuccess: Bundle downloaded — run apply.sh or apply.ps1 on your machine

users.title: Users
users.description: Admins can review every registered user, see usage stats, disable / enable accounts, and toggle admin roles.

actions.disableConfirm: Disable {{username}}? They won't be able to log in; their API keys stop working.
actions.newPasswordPlaceholder: Enter and confirm a new password

register.title: Open registration
register.description: When enabled, anyone reaching /admin/register can sign up. When disabled, only admins can create accounts from this page.
